message:
test(wills-estate): stable data-viz hooks for each visualization + expand E2E coverage Two coupled changes so the Playwright e2e spec can target each individual visualization on the wills & estate article by a stable selector rather than DOM traversal. components/viz-error-boundary.tsx - `withErrorBoundary

(Component, vizId)` now takes a required `vizId` string and wraps the dynamic-loaded visualization in a `

` around the existing `ErrorBoundary`. The five call sites below each get a kebab-case id matching the chart: `"tier-triage"`, `"axiom-coherence"`, `"intake-phases"`, `"deliverables-tree"`, `"anti-pattern-cards"`. These ids are what the e2e spec uses to wait for and assert against each chart independently. tests/e2e/wills-estate-article.spec.ts - Substantial coverage expansion (258 lines, +196/-76): - Per-viz visibility + fallback tests using the new `[data-viz=""]` selectors, so a runtime failure in any one chart is caught without taking down the others' tests. - Scroll-progress indicator assertions using the hero scroll-indicator element introduced with the recent article fleshing. - Keyboard interaction tests on the Stepper component inside IntakePhasesViz (arrow-key navigation, focus trapping). - Reduced-motion preference honored paths (the article + every chart has `useReducedMotion`-gated animation branches, and the spec now drives a `emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: 'reduce' })` case through each). Continues to skip `.beads/.write.lock` — ephemeral sqlite advisory lock, gitignored. Co-Authored-By: Claude
